title: श्री गङ्गा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गङ्गा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
नमामि गङ्गे तव पादपङ्कजं सुरासुरैर्वन्दितदिव्यरूपम्।
भुक्तिं च मुक्तिं च ददासि नित्यं भागीरथि त्वां प्रणमामि नित्यम्॥

श्वेताम्बरधरां देवीं शङ्खपद्मविभूषिताम्।
मकरवाहनारूढां गङ्गां वन्दे जगत्प्रसूम्॥

॥ सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥
ॐ गङ्गा विष्णुपदी देवी विष्णुपादाब्जसम्भवा।
हरवल्लभा शिवजटानिवासिनी पापनाशिनी॥ १॥

भागीरथी भोगवती जाह्नवी त्रिपथगामिनी।
त्रिलोकगामिनी पुण्या सर्वपापविनाशिनी॥ २॥

मन्दाकिनी अलकनन्दा धवला सुरनिम्नगा।
सुरधुनी देवनदी विश्वनाथप्रिया शुभा॥ ३॥

शिवशीर्षविहारिणी ब्रह्मलोकविनिर्गता।
वैकुण्ठधाममनिसृता त्रिदशेश्वरपूजिता॥ ४॥

सगरात्मजमोक्षदा भगीरथतपःफला।
श्रीराघवपदस्पृष्टा सीतालक्ष्मणवन्दिता॥ ५॥

नारायणी वैष्णवी च ब्राह्मी रौद्री च शाम्भवी।
सर्वदेवमयी दिव्या सर्वतीर्थमयी परा॥ ६॥

क्षीरधवला शुभ्रवर्णा शान्तरूपा मनोहरा।
कुण्डलिनी योगमाता प्राणदात्री सुखप्रदा॥ ७॥

पापहारिणी पुण्यदा मोक्षलक्ष्मीप्रदायिनी।
सर्वव्याधिविनाशिनी सर्वदुःखविमोचिनी॥ ८॥

सर्वलोकहितप्रदा सर्वभूतदयापरा।
जीवनदायिनी धात्री पोषिणी सस्यवर्धिनी॥ ९॥

सप्तसागरगामिनी ब्रह्माण्डव्यापिनी शुचिः।
शतशाखा सहस्रधारा अनन्ता परमेश्वरी॥ १०॥

हरिद्वारनिवासिनी काशीपुरविराजिता।
प्रयागसंगमस्थाना त्रिवेणी सरयूप्रिया॥ ११॥

गङ्गासागरसंयुक्ता गोमुखोद्भवशोभिता।
ऋषिकेशसुसंस्थाना बदरीनाथपादगा॥ १२॥

नित्यशुद्धा नित्यबुद्धा नित्यमुक्ता निरामया।
निराकारा निर्विकारा निर्गुणा गुणवर्धिनी॥ १३॥

प्रातःस्नानप्रसन्ना च मध्याह्नस्नानकारिणी।
सायंस्नानसुखा रात्रिस्नानसर्वार्थसाधिनी॥ १४॥

मकरसंक्रान्तिमहोत्सवा कुम्भस्नानफलप्रदा।
कार्तिकस्नानसन्तुष्टा माघस्नानवरप्रदा॥ १५॥

गयाश्राद्धफलदात्री पिण्डदानसुतोषिता।
पितृमोक्षप्रदा दिव्या तर्पणप्रीतमानसा॥ १६॥

सर्वसिद्धिप्रदा गङ्गा सर्वकामफलप्रदा।
सर्वमङ्गलदात्री च सर्वसम्पत्प्रदायिनी॥ १७॥

अमृतस्वरूपा गङ्गा जगन्माता जगद्धिता।
महापातकनाशिनी सर्वप्राणिसुखावहा॥ १८॥

महानदी पवित्राङ्गी तीर्थराजप्रयागगा।
सरस्वतीयमुनायुक्ता त्रिवेणीसंगमप्रिया॥ १९॥

सहस्रनामसम्पन्ना गङ्गा लोकसुखप्रदा।
भवार्णवतारिणी दिव्या परमानन्ददायिनी॥ २०॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
गङ्गासहस्रनामेदं यः पठेद्भक्तिसंयुतः।
सर्वपापविनिर्मुक्तो गङ्गालोकमवाप्नुयात्॥

प्रातःकाले पठेन्नित्यं गङ्गातीरे विशेषतः।
गङ्गास्नानफलं तस्य प्रतिदिनं भवेद्ध्रुवम्॥

॥ इति स्कन्दपुराणान्तर्गते श्री गङ्गा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ganga Sahasranama Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Namami Gange Tava Padapankajam Suraasurair-vandita-divya-rupam.
Bhuktim Cha Muktim Cha Dadasi Nityam Bhagirathi Tvam Pranamami Nityam.

Shvetambaradharam Devim Shankhapadmavibhushitam.
Makaravahana-arudham Gangam Vande Jagatprasum.

|| Sahasranama Stotram ||
Om Ganga Vishnupadi Devi Vishnupadabjasambhava.
Haravallabha Shivajatanivasini Papanashini. || 1 ||

Bhagirathi Bhogavati Jahnavi Tripathagamini.
Trilokagamini Punya Sarvapapa-vinashini. || 2 ||

Mandakini Alakananda Dhavala Suranimnaga.
Suradhuni Devanadi Vishvanatha-priya Shubha. || 3 ||

Shiva-shirsha-viharini Brahmaloka-vinirgata.
Vaikunthadhamanisrita Tridasheshvara-pujita. || 4 ||

Sagaratmaja-mokshada Bhagiratha-tapah-phala.
Shri-Raghava-pada-sprishta Sita-Lakshmana-vandita. || 5 ||

Narayani Vaishnavi Cha Brahmi Raudri Cha Shambhavi.
Sarvadevaamayi Divya Sarvatirthamayi Para. || 6 ||

Kshira-dhavala Shubhravarna Shanta-rupa Manohara.
Kundalini Yogamata Pranadatri Sukhaprada. || 7 ||

Papaharini Punyada Moksha-Lakshmi-pradayini.
Sarvavyadhi-vinashini Sarvaduhkha-vimochini. || 8 ||

Sarvaloka-hitaprada Sarvabhuta-dayapara.
Jivanadayini Dhatri Poshini Sasyavardhini. || 9 ||

Saptasagara-gamini Brahmandavyapini Shuchih.
Shatashakha Sahasradhara Ananta Parameshvari. || 10 ||

Haridvaranvasini Kashipura-virajita.
Prayagasamgamasthana Triveni Sarayupriya. || 11 ||

Gangasagara-samyukta Gomukhod-bhava-shobhita.
Rishikesha-susamsthana Badrinathapadaga. || 12 ||

Nityashuddha Nityabuddha Nityamukta Niramaya.
Nirakara Nirvikara Nirguna Gunavardhini. || 13 ||

Pratahsnana-prasanna Cha Madhyahnasnankarini.
Sayamsnana-sukha Ratrisnana-sarvartha-sadhini. || 14 ||

Makarasankrantimahotsava Kumbhasnana-phalaprada.
Kartikasnanasantushta Maghasnana-varaprada. || 15 ||

Gayashraddha-phaladatri Pindadana-sutoshita.
Pitrimokshaprada Divya Tarpanapritamanasa. || 16 ||

Sarvasiddhiprada Ganga Sarvakamaphalaprada.
Sarvamangaladatri Cha Sarvasampat-pradayini. || 17 ||

Amritaswarupa Ganga Jaganmata Jagaddhita.
Mahapataka-nashini Sarvaprani-sukhavaha. || 18 ||

Mahanadi Pavitraangi Tirtharaja-prayagaga.
Sarasvati-Yamunayukta Triveni-sangamapriya. || 19 ||

Sahasranama-sampanna Ganga Lokasukhaprada.
Bhavarnavatarini Divya Paramananda-dayini. || 20 ||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Gangasahasranam-idam Yah Pathed-bhakti-samyutah.
Sarvapapavinirmukto Gangalokam-avapnuyat.

Pratahkale Pathennityam Gangatire Visheshatah.
Gangasnana-phalam Tasya Pratidinam Bhaved-dhruvam.

|| Iti Skandapuranantargate Shri Ganga Sahasranama Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान: हे गङ्गे! आपके चरणकमलों को सुर-असुर वन्दन करते हैं। आप भुक्ति-मुक्ति दोनों प्रदान करती हैं। श्वेताम्बरधारी, शंख-पद्मधारी, मकरवाहन पर आरूढ, जगत् की माता गंगा को वन्दन।

१-५: गंगा विष्णुपदी, शिवजटानिवासिनी, पापनाशिनी, भागीरथी, जाह्नवी, त्रिपथगामिनी, मन्दाकिनी, अलकनन्दा, देवनदी, शिवशीर्षविहारिणी, ब्रह्मलोक से निकलीं, सगरपुत्रों को मोक्ष देने वाली, भगीरथ की तपस्या का फल हैं।

६-१०: नारायणी, सर्वदेवमयी, सर्वतीर्थमयी, पापहारिणी, मोक्षलक्ष्मीप्रदायिनी, सर्वव्याधिविनाशिनी, जीवनदायिनी, सस्यवर्धिनी, सप्तसागरगामिनी, ब्रह्माण्डव्यापिनी, सहस्रधारा, अनन्ता हैं।

११-१५: हरिद्वार, काशी, प्रयाग, त्रिवेणी, गंगासागर, गोमुख, ऋषिकेश, बदरीनाथ में विराजमान हैं। नित्यशुद्ध, नित्यमुक्त, निरामय हैं। प्रातः-मध्याह्न-सायं-रात्रि स्नान सब फलदायक है।

१६-२०: गयाश्राद्ध, पिण्डदान, पितृमोक्ष प्रदान करती हैं। सर्वसिद्धिप्रदा, सर्वमङ्गलदात्री, अमृतस्वरूपा, महापातकनाशिनी, भवार्णवतारिणी हैं।

फलश्रुति: भक्तिपूर्वक इस सहस्रनाम का पाठ करने से सर्वपापमुक्ति और गंगालोक प्राप्ति होती है। गंगातीर पर प्रातःकाल पाठ से प्रतिदिन गंगास्नान का फल मिलता है।
benefits: Contains the thousand divine names of Mother Ganga
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and grants ganga loka (divine abode of ganga)
Reciting at Ganga Tirtha at dawn grants daily Ganga Snana phala
Invokes pitru moksha - liberation for ancestors through sacred merit
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of diseases and bestows all forms of prosperity
Equivalent to bathing in Ganga when recited with devotion anywhere
description: Dedicated to Goddess Ganga, the Ganga Sahasranama Stotram (श्री गङ्गा सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्) is a sacred recitation of 1000 divine names revered in Hindu tradition. Drawn from Skanda Purana, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to remove all diseases and bestow all forms of prosperity, equivalent to bathing in Ganga when recited with devotion anywhere, and contain the thousand divine names of Mother Ganga. This prayer is traditionally recited on Monday (Somvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Ganga Dussehra, Makar Sankranti, and Kumbh Mela to invoke divine grace. The text contains 20 verses and takes around 25 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Ganga Sahasranama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Ganga Sahasranama in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. 1000 divine names of Mother Ganga for purification, liberation, and ancestral blessings.

Ganga Aarti Haridwar Varanasi Rishikesh timings
Gomukh source of Ganga Gangotri Uttarakhand
Pitru tarpan at Gaya pind daan Ganga
Magh snan Kartik snan Ganga benefits
Maa Ganga mantra for purification of sins

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ganga Sahasranama and which deity does it praise?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganga Sahasranama Stotram is the sacred 1000-name hymn to Mother Ganga preserved in the Skanda Purana, addressing her as the supreme purifier (papa-nashini), liberator (moksha-pradayini), and mother of the universe (jagad-amba). The hymn opens with a dhyana-shloka invoking Ganga seated on the makara-vahana (crocodile-mount), white-clad, holding shankha and padma. The names span her many manifestations and identities — Vishnupadi (born from Vishnu's foot), Shiva-jata-nivasini (residing in Shiva's matted hair), Bhagirathi (brought down by Bhagiratha), Jahnavi (re-emerged from sage Jahnu's ear), Tripathagamini (flowing through three worlds — heaven, earth, patala), Mandakini, Alakananda, and Triveni. (Sources: Skanda Purana; Hindupedia Ganga; Wikipedia Ganges.)

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Ganga Sahasranama and what is its source?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganga Sahasranama is preserved primarily in the Skanda Purana, with parallel versions appearing in the Padma Purana (Patala Khanda), Brahma Purana, and the Mahabharata's Anushasana Parva. The text is recited in the rishi-uttaravada framework — sage Narada or sage Markandeya narrates the names to a disciple. Several recensions circulate: the Skanda Purana version (most popular, used at Haridwar and Varanasi), the Padma Purana variant, and modern compilations published by Stotra Nidhi and Sanskrit Documents. The Adi Shankara school also preserves a Ganga Sahasranama paatha as part of the Tirtha-yatra parva. (Sources: Skanda Purana manuscripts; Padma Purana; Hindupedia.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Ganga considered the most sacred river in Hinduism?
seoFaqs.answer: Ganga is considered the most sacred river because of her unique cosmic origin and threefold flow. According to the Bhagavata Purana and Ramayana, she originated from Vishnu's foot (hence Vishnupadi) when Vamana measured the cosmos in his trivikrama stride; she then flowed through Brahmaloka. Sage Bhagiratha's thousand-year penance brought her to earth to liberate his ancestors — the 60,000 sons of King Sagara burnt by Kapila Muni's curse. To absorb her cosmic descent, Lord Shiva caught her in his matted hair (jata) at Kailasha. From there she emerged at Gangotri, flowed past Haridwar, Varanasi, Prayagraj and Gangasagar, granting bhukti and mukti to all who bathe in her or perform pitru-tarpan on her banks. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 5.17; Ramayana Bala Kanda; Skanda Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite Ganga Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: Monday (Somvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) are the prescribed weekdays. The supreme occasions are Ganga Dussehra (Jyestha Shukla Dashami, the day Ganga descended to earth — usually late May/early June), Ganga Saptami (Vaishakha Shukla Saptami, her re-emergence from Jahnu rishi), Makar Sankranti (mid-January, the great Kumbh-snana), Magha Snan and Kartik Snan months, Vaikuntha Chaturdashi, and the four Kumbh Melas held every twelve years at Haridwar, Prayagraj, Nashik and Ujjain. Pre-dawn brahma-muhurta recitation on the banks of Ganga is said to grant the merit of daily Ganga-snana. The phalashruti specifically promises that pratah-kala paatha at Ganga-tira yields daily Ganga-snana-phala. (Sources: Drikpanchang Ganga Dussehra; Skanda Purana; Kumbh Mela portal.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of pitru tarpan at Ganga?
seoFaqs.answer: Pitru tarpan (oblation to ancestors) at Ganga is considered the most efficacious form of pitru-yajna in Hindu tradition because Ganga herself is the moksha-pradayini for ancestors — her descent was secured by Bhagiratha specifically to liberate his pitrus. The sahasranama names her as Pitri-moksha-prada (granter of liberation to ancestors) and Tarpana-prita-manasa (delighted by oblation). The most efficacious pitru-karma sites on her banks are Haridwar (especially Brahmakund), Gaya (where pinda-dana is performed for three generations), Prayagraj (Triveni Sangam), Varanasi (Manikarnika Ghat), and Gangasagar (where she meets the Bay of Bengal). Ancestral merit accumulated through Ganga-tarpan is said to liberate seven generations on both sides. (Sources: Garuda Purana Pretakhanda; Skanda Purana Kashi Khanda; Hindupedia pitru-karma.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Ganga Sahasranama grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti declares that whoever recites this sahasranama with bhakti is freed from all sins (sarva-papa-vinirmuktah) and attains Ganga-loka (the divine realm of Ganga). Pre-dawn recitation on Ganga's banks grants the daily merit of Ganga-snana. Specific benefits include purification of all karmic sins including the maha-pataka (greatest five sins), liberation of seven generations of ancestors, healing of all diseases (sarva-vyadhi-vinashini), removal of all sorrows (sarva-duhkha-vimochini), bestowal of all prosperity (sarva-sampat-pradayini), fulfilment of all desires (sarva-kama-phala-prada), and ultimately moksha through bhavarnava-tarini (boat across the ocean of samsara). The recitation is said to be equivalent to Ganga-snana when performed anywhere with devotion. (Sources: Skanda Purana phalashruti; Hindupedia Ganga; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Ganga Sahasranama differ from Ganga Stotram and Ganga Lahari?
seoFaqs.answer: These three Ganga compositions vary in length and style. The Ganga Sahasranama (1000 names, 20-30 minutes) is the comprehensive archana-paatha used during full Ganga puja with bilva, tulsi and white flowers offered name by name. The Ganga Stotram refers to several short hymns — most famously Adi Shankara's Devi Sureshvari Bhagavati Gange (8 verses) — used for daily darshana and at the Haridwar/Varanasi Ganga Aarti. The Ganga Lahari ('Wave of Ganga') is the famous 52-verse Sanskrit kavya by the 17th-century poet Pandit Jagannatha — composed when he sat on each step of Manikarnika Ghat in Varanasi as Ganga rose to embrace him; it is recited as a literary devotional. Daily upasakas chant the stotram, monthly purnima the sahasranama, and the lahari as a poetic meditation. (Sources: Adi Shankara stotras; Stotra Nidhi; Pandit Jagannatha biography.)
